rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type scopes when referring to ports, and in implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Only include range assumptions and guarantees in AGREE annex when concrete bounds or visible feature names are provided.
generic_rules: Use boolean helper declarations to express range conditions in AGREE, matching the type of the expression being compared.
Ensure that AGREE contract elements reference visible identifiers or named constants from the model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Declare range checks as boolean expressions: eq var : bool = value >= min and value <= max;
Place AGREE annex blocks in valid component type or implementation scopes depending on the scope of the referenced features.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level port bindings in component type contracts unless represented through variables or outputs.
Do not include range assumptions or guarantees in AGREE unless concrete thresholds or visible feature names are available.
